rulings.Case Title: ANYSSA LERIO ET AL VS. REPRODUCTIVE MEDICINE ASSOCIATES OF NORTHERN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, Mar-13-2026. Line 2. DEFENDANTS REPRODUCTIVE MEDICINE ASSOCIATES OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, P.C., and DANIEL KASER'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant Katherine Baxter (previously Doe No. 1) demurs to specified causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Anyssa Lerio and Dionna Ruiz. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract and as Lerio's third cause of action for negligence. The demurrer is otherwise overruled. (There is a discrepancy between the numbering of the causes of action in the parties' briefs and that found in the Complaint itself. The court uses the numbers found in the Complaint.) ***THE COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO THE PARTIES*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(3012/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER JOHNSON ET AL VS. PAUL HEUBERGER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents From Defendants And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, Mar-13-2026. Line 9. PLAINTIFFS CHRISTOPHER JOHNSON, and MELANIE JOHNSON'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents From Defendants And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiffs' motion to compel further responses and production, and for sanctions, is granted in part as stated herein. This is a wrongful eviction / constructive termination case. Plaintiff Christopher Johnson seeks an order compelling production of redacted lease agreements for the subject unit after he vacated; compelling production of communications between defendants the Heubergers and their former counsel based on a waiver theory; production of communications between the Heubergers and third parties about the unit and about the plaintiffs; and compelling amendment to misnumbered responses to requests for production. ***THE COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO THE PARTIES*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CREDIBLE OPERATIONS, INC. VS. NERDWALLET, INC.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, Mar-13-2026. Line 10. MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. Plaintiff Credible Operations, Inc. seeks a preliminary injunction restraining defendant NerdWallet, Inc. from repudiating the contracts known as Insertion Orders #6 and #7 (IO6, IO7, respectively), and requiring NerdWallet to immediately restore all referral links and traffic to Credible and abide by the exclusivity clauses of IO6 and IO7. The court denies the motion for injunction, concluding that Credible has not shown it would suffer damages if preliminary injunctive relief is denied beyond what it is entitled to recover at trial. Further explanation is provided below. ***THE COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO THE PARTIES*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely.
